prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap of pyridine (energy difference between frontier orbitals), in eV.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied Cartesian geometry file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`; the structure was **not** re-optimized at DFT (`DFT//input-XYZ`).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model requested).
Electronic state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: chemkit  (backend PySCF; version as in environment).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine. Integrity check status = ok, trustworthy = true. No fallbacks or approximations beyond the chosen level of theory.
Live log / result files: main run logged to a live `.out` file during execution (path given in `cli_invocation`); final JSON written to `pyridine_frontier_b3lyp.json`.

Result
HOMO energy = –7.2525992 eV
LUMO energy = –1.112786 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap; not a true excitation energy)

No engine warnings were reported.

Therefore, the computed frontier-orbital gap of pyridine at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP (gas-phase) level is:

HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV
